step_name: Research Queue v1 schema + loader/validator (read-only, non-executable)

what_was_implemented.description: Standard-library-only, read-only loader + fail-closed validator for the research queue. It loads configs/research_queue.json, validates every item against runner/dataset/mode allowlists and a pinned-false safety-flag contract, and emits a normalized deterministic report. It executes nothing, imports no runner, and writes only the opt-in build report.
public_surface: write_build_report(base, report) -> [paths]  (opt-in; writes ONLY to reports/strategy_factory_queue_v1_build/)
validator_rules: required-field presence: missing any item field -> blocked_reason (item blocked, never executed)
runner allowlist: any runner not in ALLOWED_RUNNERS -> blocked
dataset allowlist: any dataset not in ALLOWED_DATASETS -> blocked
mode allowlist: any mode not in ALLOWED_MODES -> blocked
safety flags: research_only must be true; every other flag (incl. execution_authorized) must be present and false; any forbidden flag true -> blocked
execution_authorized=true (flag OR top-level field) -> blocked AND never executable
approved_for_research must be a bool; even when true it confers ONLY research-listing eligibility, never execution
executable is ALWAYS false in v1 (invariant, not computed from input)
deterministic ordering by (priority, task_id); sorted-key JSON; no wall-clock dependence
missing/corrupt file -> empty fail-closed report with a warning, never an exception
what_was_implemented.separation_of_approval: approved_for_research (queue-listing eligibility) is kept strictly separate from execution. There is NO execution authorization path in v1: execution_authorized must be false on every item, and 'executable' is pinned false by construction.

builds_on.mapping: Implements roadmap Step 2 (Research Queue v1). The registry (Step 1) is the read-only single source of truth describing EXISTING experiments; the queue (Step 2) is the human-authored, approved BACKLOG of research tasks that MAY later be considered by an orchestrator (Step 3, not built). Item field set, the nine pinned-false safety flags, and the fail-closed posture match plan section 9. No orchestrator and no execution are implemented here.

routine_layer_reuse.reused_conventions: stdlib-only, deterministic, repo-root-relative, fail-closed style of tools/strategy_report_registry.py (Step 1): same _repo_root(), _load_json-style fail-closed reads, sorted-key to_stable_json, opt-in write_build_report confined to a single build folder, and pinned-false safety flags.
routine_layer_reuse.not_reused: No runner, orchestrator, or execution code is imported or created. The validator is a separate single-purpose module so the registry scanner stays untouched.
routine_layer_reuse.duplication_avoided: New loader is minimal and single-purpose; it mirrors the Step-1 safety posture rather than forking a parallel system.

test_results.command: cd tests && PYTHONPATH=repo .venv python -m pytest test_strategy_factory_queue.py test_strategy_report_registry.py --rootdir=. -q
covered_cases: valid queue loads -> item valid, listed, but executable=false
missing required field -> item blocked (recorded, not raised)
unknown runner / unknown dataset / unknown mode -> item blocked
every forbidden safety flag true (8 parametrized incl. execution_authorized) -> item blocked
execution_authorized=true -> rejected, not executable, not listing-eligible
approved_for_research=true -> listing eligible but still not executable
no subprocess / no stray writes; no runner module imported
deterministic ordering by (priority, task_id) + byte-identical JSON
missing/corrupt queue file fails closed with a warning (no crash)
SHIPPED configs/research_queue.json validates; N=20 task appears but is non-executable

safety_confirmations.lane_unchanged: Crypto-D1 remains WATCH / MIXED and NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA

next_step.name: Research Orchestrator v1 (DRY-RUN ONLY, plan-emitting)
next_step.deliverable: A read-only orchestrator that joins the Step-1 registry with the Step-2 queue and emits a DRY-RUN execution plan (what WOULD run, in what order, against which frozen dataset) WITHOUT running anything. Dry-run by default; an 'execute' mode must be a separate, explicitly approved build behind the safety-gate contract. No backtest, no dataset mutation, no runner call until separately approved.

non_authorization_statement: This build adds a research-queue schema, a read-only fail-closed loader/validator, and its tests only. It authorizes no paper or live trading, no broker/exchange/order/fetch path, no ACTIVE/STRONG promotion, and no Bundle 23. It executes no queue item, runs no backtest, imports no runner, builds no orchestrator, and mutates no dataset. Every queue item is non-executable by construction. Crypto-D1 remains WATCH / MIXED and NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA.
